content:
# John Chapter 6 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/JHN_006_page_001.jpg, JHN_006_page_002.jpg, JHN_006_page_003.jpg

1. After these things the Yeshua went away to the other side of the sea of the Galilaia of Tiberias.

2. And a great crowd was following him, because they were seeing the signs that he was doing upon the sick.

3. And the Yeshua went up on the mountain, and there he was sitting with the disciples of him.

4. And the Pascha was near, the feast of the Ioudaioi.

5. Then the Yeshua, having lifted up the eyes and having seen that a great crowd comes to him, says to Philippos, "From where shall we buy bread that these may eat?"

6. But this he was saying testing him, for he himself knew what he was about to do.

7. Philippos answered him, "Two hundred denarii of bread is not sufficient for them, that each may receive a little."

8. One of the disciples of him, Andreas the brother of Simon Petros, says to him,

9. "There is a little boy here who has five barley loaves and two fish; but what are these for so many?"

10. The Yeshua said, "Make the people recline." And there was much grass in the place. Then the men reclined, the number about five thousand.

11. Then the Yeshua took the loaves, and having given thanks, distributed to the [ones] reclining; likewise also from the fish, as much as they wished.

12. And when they were filled, he says to the disciples of him, "Gather the fragments having been left over, that nothing may be lost."

13. Then they gathered and filled twelve baskets of fragments from the five barley loaves which were left over by the [ones] having eaten.

14. Then the people, having seen what sign he did, said, "This is truly the Prophet, the [one] coming into the world."

15. Then the Yeshua, knowing that they are about to come and seize him that they might make him king, withdrew again to the mountain, himself alone.

16. And when evening came, the disciples of him went down to the sea,

17. and having entered into a boat, they were going to the other side of the sea to Kapernaoum. And darkness had already come, and the Yeshua had not yet come to them.

18. And the sea was being stirred up by a great wind blowing.

19. Then having rowed about twenty-five or thirty stadia, they see the Yeshua walking upon the sea and coming near the boat, and they were afraid.

20. But he says to them, "I am; do not fear."

21. Then they wished to receive him into the boat, and immediately the boat came to be at the land to which they were going.

22. On the morrow the crowd, the [one] standing on the other side of the sea, saw that there was no other boat there except one, and that the Yeshua did not enter with the disciples of him into the boat, but the disciples of him alone went away.

23. But other boats came from Tiberias near the place where they ate the bread, the Lord having given thanks.

24. When therefore the crowd saw that the Yeshua is not there nor the disciples of him, they themselves entered into the boats and came to Kapernaoum, seeking the Yeshua.

25. And having found him on the other side of the sea, they said to him, "Rabbi, when did you come here?"

26. The Yeshua answered them and said, "Truly, truly I say to you, you seek me not because you saw signs, but because you ate of the loaves and were filled.

27. Work not for the food perishing, but for the food remaining into eternal life, which the Son of the Man will give to you, for this [one] the Father sealed — the God."

28. Then they said to him, "What should we do that we may work the works of the God?"

29. The Yeshua answered and said to them, "This is the work of the God, that you believe in the [one] whom that [one] sent."

30. Then they said to him, "What then sign do you do, that we may see and believe you? What do you work?

31. The fathers of us ate the manna in the wilderness, just as it has been written, 'Bread from the heaven he gave to them to eat.'"

32. Then the Yeshua said to them, "Truly, truly I say to you, Mouses did not give to you the bread from the heaven, but the Father of me gives to you the true bread from the heaven.

33. For the bread of the God is the [one] coming down from the heaven and giving life to the world."

34. Then they said to him, "Lord, always give to us this bread."

35. The Yeshua said to them, "I am the bread of the life. The [one] coming to me will not hunger, and the [one] believing in me will never thirst.

36. But I said to you that you have also seen me and do not believe.

37. All that the Father gives to me will come to me, and the [one] coming to me I will not cast out,

38. because I have come down from the heaven not that I should do the will of me, but the will of the [one] having sent me.

39. And this is the will of the [one] having sent me, that all that he has given to me I should not lose from it, but should raise it up on the last day.

40. For this is the will of the Father of me, that everyone seeing the Son and believing in him may have eternal life, and I will raise him up on the last day."

41. Then the Ioudaioi were grumbling about him because he said, "I am the bread having come down from the heaven."

42. And they said, "Is this not Yeshua, the son of Ioseph, whose father and mother we know? How does he now say, 'From the heaven I have come down'?"

43. The Yeshua answered and said to them, "Do not grumble with one another.

44. No one is able to come to me unless the Father having sent me draws him, and I will raise him up on the last day.

45. It has been written in the prophets, 'And they will all be taught of God.' Everyone having heard from the Father and having learned comes to me.

46. Not that anyone has seen the Father, except the [one] being from the God — this [one] has seen the Father.

47. Truly, truly I say to you, the [one] believing has eternal life.

48. I am the bread of the life.

49. The fathers of you ate the manna in the wilderness, and they died.

50. This is the bread coming down from the heaven, that someone may eat of it and not die.

51. I am the living bread having come down from the heaven. If anyone eats of this bread, he will live into the age. And the bread that I will give is the flesh of me for the life of the world."

52. Then the Ioudaioi were disputing with one another, saying, "How is this [one] able to give us the flesh of him to eat?"

53. Then the Yeshua said to them, "Truly, truly I say to you, unless you eat the flesh of the Son of the Man and drink the blood of him, you do not have life in yourselves.

54. The [one] eating the flesh of me and drinking the blood of me has eternal life, and I will raise him up on the last day.

55. For the flesh of me is true food, and the blood of me is true drink.

56. The [one] eating the flesh of me and drinking the blood of me remains in me, and I in him.

57. Just as the living Father sent me, and I live because of the Father, so the [one] eating me — that [one] also will live because of me.

58. This is the bread having come down from heaven — not as the fathers ate and died. The [one] eating this bread will live into the age."

59. These things he said in [the] synagogue, teaching in Kapernaoum.

60. Then many of the disciples of him, having heard, said, "This word is hard; who is able to hear it?"

61. And the Yeshua, knowing in himself that the disciples of him grumble about this, said to them, "Does this scandalize you?

62. [What] then if you see the Son of the Man ascending to where he was before?

63. The Spirit is the [one] making alive; the flesh profits nothing. The words that I have spoken to you are spirit and are life.

64. But there are [some] of you who do not believe." For the Yeshua knew from [the] beginning who are the [ones] not believing and who is the [one] who will deliver him up.

65. And he said, "Because of this I have said to you that no one is able to come to me unless it has been given to him from the Father."

66. From this many of the disciples of him went away to the [things] behind and were no longer walking with him.

67. Then the Yeshua said to the twelve, "Do you also wish to go away?"

68. Simon Petros answered him, "Lord, to whom shall we go? You have words of eternal life.

69. And we have believed and have known that you are the Holy [One] of the God."

70. The Yeshua answered them, "Did I not choose you, the twelve? And of you, one is a devil."

71. And he was speaking of Ioudas [son] of Simon Iskariotes, for this [one] was about to deliver him up, being one of the twelve.

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **v.19 — "twenty-five or thirty stadia":** About 3-3.5 miles (5-6 km). The Sea of Galilee is about 7 miles wide at its broadest point. The disciples were approximately halfway across.

2. **v.20 — "I am":** ἐγώ εἰμι — on one level simply "It's me!" (self-identification). On a deeper Johannine level, the divine name (cf. Exodus 3:14). The context — walking on the sea — echoes God's mastery over the waters (Job 9:8, Psalm 77:19).

3. **v.31 — "Bread from heaven":** The crowd quotes Psalm 78:24 (or Exodus 16:4, Nehemiah 9:15). Jesus reinterprets: the true bread from heaven is not manna but himself.

4. **v.51 — "the flesh of me for the life of the world":** τὴν σάρκα μου ὑπὲρ τῆς τοῦ κόσμου ζωῆς — the preposition ὑπέρ ("for/on behalf of") introduces sacrificial language. Jesus' flesh is given sacrificially for the world's life.

5. **v.69 — "the Holy One of the God":** Sinaiticus reads ὁ ἅγιος τοῦ θεοῦ ("the Holy One of God"), not "the Christ, the Son of the living God" (as in Matthew 16:16) or "the Christ, the Son of God" found in some Johannine manuscripts. This is a distinctive Sinaiticus/Alexandrian reading — a messianic title with demonic recognition overtones (cf. Mark 1:24).

— EMET, AI Translator
